risks.title: 22 confirmed dead exports across tools and router packages
risks.evidence: static-analysis.json deadCode: tools package 17 unused exports (ghReviewTool, ghActionsTool, ghReleaseTool, ghSearchTool, ghSecurityTool, ghRepoTool, gitBranchTool, gitStashTool, costEstimateTool, createToolSearchTool, planPreviewTool, processSpawnTool, processKillTool, undoTool, checkSandbox, multiEditTool, batchEditTool, createTimeoutController); router package 5 unused exports (costFirstStrategy, qualityFirstStrategy, createRuleBasedStrategy, createCombinedStrategy, capabilityStrategy) = 83% of router's primary routing API is dead code
risks.remediation: Delete or wire the 22 dead exports. For routing strategies, confirm whether Thompson sampling alone is intended or if multi-strategy fallback is a planned feature. Cannot defer — dead code in a security-critical tool package is an audit liability.

risks.title: 11 of 26 packages (42%) have zero test files
risks.evidence: static-analysis.json codeInventory: gateway(0), ingest(0), onboard(0), orchestrator(0), plugin-sdk(0), projects(0), scheduler(0), sdk(0), server(0), vscode(0), docgen(0) all have testLines=0. dynamic-testing.json confirms all 11 have files=0, passed=0.
risks.remediation: Add minimum smoke tests for server (health endpoint), gateway (structured response from API call), and scheduler (job registers and triggers). These three are highest operational risk.

risks.title: server.ts (1450 lines, HTTP attack surface) has zero tests
risks.evidence: static-analysis.json codeInventory: server srcLines=1450, testLines=0. POST /api/v1/god-mode/execute — the primary externally-consumed endpoint — has never been tested for authentication bypass, injection, or SSRF.
risks.remediation: Add 3 integration tests to server.ts: (1) unauthenticated request returns 401, (2) JWT alg:none rejected, (3) expired token rejected. Must cross process boundary with usesRealIO=true.

risks.title: Empty jwtSecret produces startup warning instead of process exit
risks.evidence: security.json owaspFindings A05: 'packages/server/src/server.ts line 113: explicit warn logged if jwtSecret is empty at startup — message references SERVER EXPOSED WITHOUT AUTH'. Server starts and God Mode execute endpoint accepts requests when jwtSecret is unset.
risks.remediation: Change server.ts line 113 from console.warn to process.exit(1). Add a CI pre-flight check that validates required environment variables before server start.

risks.title: No production deployment — MTTR=null, all DORA metrics are commit-velocity not operational
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json dora.mttrHours=null, mttrNotes='No revert incidents found to measure MTTR from'. deployFrequency.notes='All commits to main via first-parent' — not deployment events. Median lead time 112 hours is DORA Low tier.
risks.remediation: Deploy to staging environment. Record first real deploy timestamp. Instrument actual deployment pipeline separate from git commit timestamps. MTTR requires at least one measured recovery event.

risks.title: Unbounded taskEventQueue at loop.ts:242 — 170 unbounded push calls in core
risks.evidence: architecture.json scalabilityBottlenecks: 'taskEventQueue is an unbounded Array with .push(); no max-size cap; drained via shift() in the yield loop' at packages/core/src/agent/loop.ts:242. '170 unbounded .push() calls across core/src identified; hot paths include agent loop stream parts and tool result accumulation'.
risks.remediation: Cap taskEventQueue at configurable maximum (default 1000). Emit a backpressure event when cap is hit. This is a 10-line fix with immediate memory safety impact.

risks.title: Zero runbooks — verified absent by grep across docs/
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json prr.runbooks=false, runbooksEvidence='No files matching *runbook*, *playbook*, or *ops* found in docs/'. 788 commits and 0 operational procedures.
risks.remediation: Write 3 minimum runbooks: (1) startup and health verification, (2) vault unlock failure recovery, (3) API key rotation. Each should reference the monitoring-manifest.json monitor that fires for the scenario.

risks.title: property-based tests = 0 and mutation testing unavailable
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json: propertyBasedTests=0, mutationTestingAvailable=false. Thompson sampling algorithm, vault cryptographic operations, and JWT validation have no invariant tests across the input space.
risks.remediation: Add fast-check property tests for: (1) router Thompson sampling — expected reward always [0,1], total trials monotonically increases; (2) vault round-trip — decrypt(encrypt(x)) === x for any string; (3) JWT — alg:none always rejected.

risks.title: 21 Docker-dependent tool tests skipped in CI
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json totals: skipped=21, all in @brainst0rm/tools. These are the Docker sandbox isolation tests — the security boundary preventing agent code execution escapes is not verified in standard CI.
risks.remediation: Add a separate CI job with Docker-in-Docker enabled that runs the 21 skipped tests. Process isolation under concurrent load is not a luxury test for a tool-execution engine.

risks.title: 5 synchronous IO hot paths block the Node.js event loop
risks.evidence: architecture.json scalabilityBottlenecks: (1) packages/core/src/memory/dream-runner.ts — readFileSync, writeFileSync, statSync, readdirSync, unlinkSync, mkdirSync; (2) packages/core/src/memory/manager.ts — same; (3) packages/cli/src/bin/brainstorm.ts — execFileSync for git; (4) packages/cli/src/init/detect.ts — existsSync, readFileSync; (5) packages/cli/src/init/generate.ts — existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync.
risks.remediation: Convert dream-runner.ts and memory/manager.ts file operations to async fs/promises. These are the highest-frequency paths in multi-session scenarios. CLI init paths (detect.ts, generate.ts) are startup-time and lower priority.

risks.title: Code coverage measurement disabled — 661 tests provide no measurable line coverage
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json: coverageEnabled=false. 661 passing tests confirm behavior at tested paths only; which branches are exercised is unknown.
risks.remediation: Enable vitest coverage with v8 provider. Set minimum thresholds: 60% line coverage for packages/core and packages/router as first milestone. Run once to establish baseline before setting targets.

risks.title: 3 cross-layer architecture violations constrain independent layer evolution
risks.evidence: architecture.json layers: (1) mcp -> tools (Infrastructure imports Domain), (2) core -> agents (Core imports Domain), (3) core -> tools (Core imports Domain). Core and Domain cannot be independently released. core->tools creates a potential security concern: internal tool calls may bypass the security middleware pipeline.
risks.remediation: Fix core->agents first: abstract agent profile lookup behind an interface in shared, removing the Domain import from Core. Then fix core->tools. Fix mcp->tools last. Each fix is independent.

risks.title: DaemonController uses isPaused flag AND status string — dual state representation
risks.evidence: architecture.json stateMachines DaemonController: 'isPaused flag used alongside status string; sleepUntil timestamp for timed sleep state'. Concurrent state transitions can diverge these two representations, producing undefined shutdown behavior.
risks.remediation: Eliminate the isPaused boolean. Use status string exclusively and derive isPaused as a getter. Eliminates the class of bugs where isPaused=true but status='running'.

risks.title: BaseConnector is an unused export in godmode — connector base class has no importers
risks.evidence: static-analysis.json deadCode godmode: ['BaseConnector']. The declared base class of the connector framework has 0 importers. Either connectors do not extend it (fragmented contract) or the framework was redesigned without removing the base class.
risks.remediation: Investigate why BaseConnector has 0 importers. If connectors extend a different base, update the class hierarchy or delete BaseConnector. If the framework is not used as designed, document the actual connector pattern.

risks.title: SQLite single-writer synchronous backend constrains concurrent write scaling
risks.evidence: architecture.json scalabilityBottlenecks: 'WAL mode enabled (journal_mode = WAL), foreign_keys = ON; better-sqlite3 is synchronous and single-process — no concurrent write scaling'. Any multi-session daemon mode will serialize all writes through a single event loop.
risks.remediation: Add a write queue abstraction over the SQLite client so concurrent writes are serialized without blocking the event loop. This enables future migration to a multi-writer backend without changing consumer code.

risks.title: red-team.test.ts has testsErrors=false — adversarial tests do not assert blocked outcomes
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json testQuality: packages/core/src/__tests__/red-team.test.ts testsErrors=false, packages/core/src/__tests__/red-team-engine.test.ts testsErrors=false. An adversarial test file that never asserts on error conditions is testing that the system does something, not that it correctly blocks attacks.
risks.remediation: Rewrite red-team.test.ts so every test case asserts a specific blocked outcome (testsErrors must be true). Test should confirm that adversarial input produces a specific error type or blocked result, not just that it runs without crashing.

risks.title: Monitoring manifest is setup instructions, not deployed monitors
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json monitoring.monitoringManifestEvidence: '11 monitors with UptimeRobot/BetterUptime setup instructions, Slack webhook alerting'. The manifest documents what to deploy but no evidence any monitor is active.
risks.remediation: Deploy the 11 monitoring-manifest monitors following the existing setup instructions. Verify the Slack webhook URL is active by sending a test alert. Verify Sentry DSN is configured and receiving events.

risks.title: No SLO definition documents — 45 code metric matches are not SLO documents
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json monitoring.slosDefined=false, slosEvidence='SLO grep returned 45 files but matches are metric/threshold names in code, not SLO definition documents'.
risks.remediation: Define 3 SLO documents (not code comments): CLI startup time < 5s, vault unlock < 30s per session, first chat token latency < 10s. Link each SLO to a monitor in monitoring-manifest.json.

risks.title: IPC documentation lives in inline comments, not a versioned schema
risks.evidence: architecture.json apiContracts: ipcDocumentationSource='packages/cli/src/ipc/handler.ts (inline comments)'. 23 IPC methods have no standalone versioned schema. Desktop/web app consumers cannot detect drift.
risks.remediation: Extract IPC method list to a standalone ipc-schema.json or ipc-protocol.ts with request/response type definitions. Import this from both handler.ts and the desktop IPC client.

risks.title: No automated dependency update tooling — 0-CVE state is point-in-time only
risks.evidence: Cross-exam challenge 3: 'no automated Dependabot or Renovate configuration found in evidence'. The 0-CVE state from npm audit will decay as new CVEs are disclosed without automated scanning.
risks.remediation: Add Dependabot or Renovate configuration with a policy that high/critical CVE PRs block merge. This maintains the current 0-CVE status automatically.

risks.title: God Mode contract dual-sourced in manifest.ts and platform-contract-v1.md with no drift detection
risks.evidence: architecture.json apiContracts: godModeContractSource='packages/godmode/src/manifest.ts + docs/platform-contract-v1.md'. openapi=false means drift between implementation and documentation is undetectable without manual inspection.
risks.remediation: Add a CI step that compares route handlers in server.ts against the contract manifest and fails if endpoints are added or removed without updating the manifest.

risks.title: No on-call tooling and no incident response documentation
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json prr.onCall=false, onCallEvidence='No pagerduty or opsgenie references found'. prr.incidentResponse=false, incidentResponseEvidence='No files matching *incident* or *postmortem* found in docs/'.
risks.remediation: At minimum, define a contact rotation document and link it to the monitoring-manifest Slack channel. Write one postmortem template for future incidents.

risks.title: vault test takes 17,730ms — 3.8x the next-slowest package
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json testResults: @brainst0rm/vault durationMs=17730 vs @brainst0rm/core durationMs=4660. Likely Argon2id KDF at OWASP parameters. security.json vaultSystem.autoLock='30-minute inactivity timer' suggests session-scoped not per-request.
risks.remediation: Verify vault unlock is called once per session, not per-request. If KDF is per-session, the 17s is acceptable. If per-request, reduce test parameters or add KDF parameter override for test environments.

risks.title: shared package has 106 import sites — any breaking change requires 106 updates
risks.evidence: static-analysis.json dependencyGraph: shared importedByCount=106. architecture.json coupling: shared ca=24, instability=0.00.
risks.remediation: Audit shared package for scope creep. Consider splitting into shared/types and shared/utils to reduce per-change blast radius. Add @microsoft/api-extractor to CI to detect breaking changes.

risks.title: IPC integration tests labeled isUnitTest=false but use no real IO — no process-boundary test exists
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json testQuality: packages/cli/src/__tests__/ipc/integration.test.ts isUnitTest=false, usesRealIO=false. A test labeled integration that crosses no process boundary and uses no real IO cannot catch serialization bugs or process-start failures.
risks.remediation: Convert ipc/integration.test.ts to spawn an actual CLI process and send NDJSON over a real pipe. Current test cannot detect IPC protocol drift.

risks.title: 80% of test files (37/46) do not test error conditions
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json testQuality: 37 of 46 files have testsErrors=false. The agent loop integration test (loop.integration.test.ts), KAIROS-BR loop test (kairos-br-loop.test.ts), and kairos-integration.test.ts all have testsErrors=false.
risks.remediation: Add error-path tests to loop.integration.test.ts covering provider 503, tool crash, and context overflow scenarios. Add SIGTERM shutdown tests to kairos-br-loop.test.ts.

risks.title: No mid-migration kill test — SQLite WAL atomicity under SIGKILL is assumed not verified
risks.evidence: No test exercises: open transaction, apply partial schema change, SIGKILL, restart, verify pre-migration state. Rollback code exists in transaction.ts and db/client.ts but the adversarial scenario is untested.
risks.remediation: Write the mid-migration kill test: start DB transaction, apply half the schema changes, send SIGKILL, restart, assert schema is at the pre-migration version. This validates the WAL atomicity guarantee under adversarial conditions.

risks.title: orchestrator, hooks, and scheduler have ca=0 — no static import confirmation of runtime registration
risks.evidence: architecture.json coupling: orchestrator ca=0, hooks ca=0, scheduler ca=0. The wiring audit confirms runOrchestrationPipeline and DaemonController by call site; hooks lifecycle system (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart) is not in the 12-function wiring audit.
risks.remediation: Add HookManager registration to the wiring audit. Confirm that lifecycle hooks fire at runtime by adding one integration test that registers a hook and verifies it executes on a tool call.

risks.title: No OpenAPI specification for God Mode endpoints
risks.evidence: architecture.json apiContracts: openapi=false, graphql=false, grpc=false. Third-party product connector implementations must read TypeScript source to understand the contract. Silent drift is undetectable.
risks.remediation: Generate OpenAPI 3.1 spec from server.ts route handlers. Add a CI step that fails if route signatures change without updating the spec.

risks.title: DORA lead time 112 hours (4.7 days) — DORA Low tier
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json dora.leadTime.medianHours=112. DORA Elite threshold is < 1 hour. Single-contributor project — this is self-review latency, not bottleneck from external review. Indicates large PRs with multi-day batch sizes.
risks.remediation: Reduce PR batch size. Aim for multiple small commits per day rather than large multi-day batches. Instrument actual deployment timestamps to get real DORA metrics.

risks.title: 8 state machines defined — ChangeSet has no test for executed->failed invalid transition
risks.evidence: architecture.json stateMachines: ChangeSet has 4 states (pending, approved, rejected, executed). workflow/confidence.test.ts testsErrors=true but ChangeSet itself has no invalid-transition test. A ChangeSet stuck in 'approved' after a failed execute has no recovery path tested.
risks.remediation: Write ChangeSet state machine tests covering all invalid transitions (12 invalid pairs for 4 states). Specifically test: approved->executed throws on execution failure, leaving ChangeSet in a recoverable state.

risks.title: egress-monitor domain allowlist content unverified at runtime
risks.evidence: security.json owaspFindings A10: 'egress-monitor enforces domain allowlist for POST/upload operations. Non-allowlisted domain POSTs blocked.' No evidence the allowlist is non-empty at runtime or cannot be cleared via crafted config mutation.
risks.remediation: Add a startup check that verifies egress-monitor domain allowlist is non-empty when configured. Add a test that verifies a crafted config mutation cannot clear the allowlist.

risks.title: No deployed dashboards — operational observability is blind
risks.evidence: operational-readiness.json monitoring.dashboards=false, dashboardsEvidence='No Grafana, Datadog, or other dashboard config files found'.
risks.remediation: Create a minimal Grafana dashboard JSON or Datadog monitor YAML covering the 11 monitors already defined in monitoring-manifest.json. Even a single dashboard is better than none.

risks.title: mcp package has cross-layer violation (Infrastructure imports Domain) and instability=0.67
risks.evidence: architecture.json layers Infrastructure crossLayerViolations: 'mcp -> tools (Domain): mcp imports @brainst0rm/tools'. architecture.json coupling: mcp instability=0.67 — an Infrastructure package should have low instability.
risks.remediation: Extract the tools dependency from mcp into an injected interface so mcp remains in the Infrastructure layer with instability < 0.5.

risks.title: Vault test 17.7s — if vault.unlock() is called per-operation rather than per-session, KDF becomes request-time overhead
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json: @brainst0rm/vault durationMs=17730. security.json vaultSystem.autoLock='30-minute inactivity timer' implies session-scoped, but this assumption is unverified in tests.
risks.remediation: Add a test that calls vault.get('key') twice in the same session and asserts the second call does not re-derive the key (< 100ms for second call). Documents the per-session KDF contract explicitly.

risks.title: no-mock test pattern across 43/45 files — test assumptions depend on real implementations that may not exist in CI
risks.evidence: dynamic-testing.json testQuality: 43 of 45 files show usesMocks=false. Tests that use no mocks and no real IO may be testing in-memory stubs that do not reflect production behavior.
risks.remediation: Audit the 43 no-mock files to confirm they test real code paths. The combination of no-mocks AND no-real-IO is only valid when the test exercises actual logic without external dependencies.

risks.title: subagent privilege reduction is present in code but has no test verifying ceiling enforcement under adversarial tool requests
risks.evidence: security.json owaspFindings A01: 'allowedTools intersected with parent's available tools (privilege ceiling at line ~233). MUTATING_TYPES (code, general) downgraded to read-only without explicit permissionCheck'. No test sends an adversarial tool request that attempts to exceed the subagent's privilege ceiling.
risks.remediation: Add a test that creates a subagent, attempts to invoke a tool not in the parent's allowed set, and asserts the request is rejected with a privilege-ceiling error.

risks.title: Dead code in core package — 7 unused exports including LoopDetector and SessionPatternLearner
risks.evidence: static-analysis.json deadCode core: ['normalizeInsightMarkers', 'LoopDetector', 'SessionPatternLearner', 'FileWatcher', 'classifyPlanTask', 'estimateTaskCost', 'loadIgnorePatterns'].
risks.remediation: Review and remove or mark internal the 7 unused core exports. LoopDetector and SessionPatternLearner are named features that should either be wired or removed with a note explaining why they were deprecated.
